commands.repair_reason: There is a narrow repairable blocker in the PR branch: make restore provenance-aware and fix the changelog reference.; Needs attention: No dependency or workflow supply-chain changes were found, but the restore condition can keep stale memory corpus adapters active after a plugin stops registering them.; Review finding: [P2] Avoid restoring dropped service-plugin supplements — src/plugins/loader.ts:2434-2435 - [P3]... commands.review_summary:
The PR snapshots memory corpus supplements before activating plugin reloads, restores non-re-registered supplements for service-owning plugins, adds loader regression tests, and adds a changelog entry.

Reproducibility: yes. source-level. Current main clears memory plugin state on activating loads, service.start registrations happen after registry loading, and lazy registry loading can re-run register() without starting services, matching the linked #77039 trace.
commands.review_followup: There is a narrow repairable blocker in the PR branch: make restore provenance-aware and fix the changelog reference.

target.title: fix(plugins): preserve corpus supplements from service.start() during activating re-loads
target.author: hclsys
